title: castilleja applegatei subsp. disticha
intro: Castilleja applegatei subsp. disticha, also known as mountain Indian paintbrush, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by connecting its roots to the roots of surrounding plants, which it then absorbs nutrients from. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues such as sore throats, wounds, and skin conditions. Castilleja applegatei subsp. disticha can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to reap its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
